title: The rights and responsibilities of citizenship: level 2
description: Test how well you can apply your knowledge on the rights, responsibilities, and obligations of citizenship!
topic_path: What are the rights and responsibilities of citizenship?
The rights and responsibilities of citizenship: level 2

assessment_items.question_content:
Risa got into college after graduating from high school. To go to the University of Washington, Risa relocated across the country from Knoxville, Tennessee to Seattle, Washington.

 **What right did Risa exercise?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: freedom of movement
freedom of expression
right to privacy
right to choose your own employment

assessment_items.question_content:
Santino injures his arm at a softball game and goes to a doctor to get it checked out. The doctor tells him he probably shouldn’t go to his regular job as a construction worker because it would probably make the injury worse. When Santino refuses to take time off, his doctor threatens to tell his boss. Santino reminds his doctor that she can’t do that without violating his rights. 

**What right is Santino referring to?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to privacy
right to access healthcare
right to choose one’s work
right to access social security

assessment_items.question_content:
Kiara wants to convert to Judaism. She’s started taking classes at a local synagogue to learn more Judaism before she starts the formal conversion process. 

**What right did Kiara exercise?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: freedom of religion
right to access education
freedom of assembly
right to change employment

assessment_items.question_content:
To prepare for the 2020 election, Morgan started researching political parties and their candidates to determine who he wanted to support. After looking through the party platforms, Morgan joins the Libertarian Party and supports their candidates.

 **What right did Morgan exercise?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: freedom of association
right to vote
right to join a labor union
freedom of movement

assessment_items.question_content:
Camden is running for a position on his school board. He feels like the current school board members have been out of school for so long that they are out of touch with the issues affecting current high school students. 

**What right did Camden exercise?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to run for public office
right to vote
right to choose one’s employment
right to join a political party

assessment_items.question_content:
Paola is frustrated with her governor’s veto of a law that would lower taxes on small business owners like herself. To voice her displeasure, she coordinates with several hundred business owners in the state of Iowa to march on the capitol. 

**What right did Paola exercise?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: freedom of assembly
freedom of press
right to join professional organizations
right to join a political party

assessment_items.question_content:
Tilde decides she wants to build a cabin out in the woods about an hour away from her house. She goes to the bank to get a loan and then goes to the real estate office to purchase the land. After getting the necessary equipment and hiring some people to help, she starts construction. 

**What right did Tilde exercise?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to acquire and use property
right to just compensation for the taking of private property for public use
freedom of movement
freedom of speech

assessment_items.question_content:
Tsedenia writes a song to showcase on a reality talent show. Before she does that she wants to protect her work so that no one can steal it and claim it as their own. 

**What right should Tsedenia exercise to protect her work?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to copyright assignment
right to acquire property
right to enter lawful contracts
right to choose one’s work

assessment_items.question_content:
Wei loves his job as a nurse at a large hospital, but the pay is too low to support his family and he works long hours. He applies for a new job to serve as a nurse at a private practice to get a higher paycheck and better hours. 

**What right does Wei exercise?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to change employment
right to join professional associations
right to earn a wage
right to work in safe conditions

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following activities is an example of a person performing a civic responsibility rather than an obligation?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Baako watches his local news every night to keep up-to-date on issues affecting his community.
Celeste joins a labor union to fight for better working conditions in her workplace.
Paxton registers for the selective service when he turns eighteen.
Claudia canvasses her neighborhood to encourage people to vote for Representative Loftis for a state senate seat.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following activities is an example of a person performing a civic responsibility rather than an obligation?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Naomi attends a town hall meeting to talk to her local representatives about the increasing number of potholes around town.
Enzo submits all the necessary tax information to the federal government by April 15th.
Ming runs for a seat in the House of Representatives on behalf of her political party.
Gian registers his son for kindergarten at a local elementary school when he turns five.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following activities is an example of a person performing a civic responsibility rather than an obligation?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Bodhi votes in the 2022 midterm election.
Elliott joins the Air Force after graduating from high school.
Chira has to serve on a jury for a major kidnapping case.
Miriam registers with the Democratic Party.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following activities is an example of an obligation rather than a civic responsibility?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sheng registers for the selective service after graduating from high school.
Talia votes for her cousin in her town’s mayoral election.
Kofi volunteers at a local animal shelter on his free weekends.
Isidora joins the American Medical Association after getting her Doctor of Medicine.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following activities is an example of an obligation rather than a civic responsibility?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Desta shows up to the courthouse after getting a summons for jury service.
Eric moves to the nation’s capital after accepting an offer to work for the president.
Clara files for a patent with the federal government after creating a device that monitors sugar levels.
Regina swears on a Bible at her inauguration ceremony for the presidency.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following activities is an example of an obligation rather than a civic responsibility?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Liu sends all of her tax information to the government by April 15th.
Zainab attends a meet and greet with her town’s mayor to learn more about her plans for the year.
Omar plants several trees in the local park as a part of a community environmental program.
Sergio reads the local newspaper to learn about an initiative on the ballot for the next election.

assessment_items.question_content:
> **Madison, WI** — Yesterday, a group of about 275 people met outside of the Roosevelt Heights Community Center to protest city plans to tear down the center to open up the coveted space for commercial businesses.

>“This community center is important to our neighborhood,” Rowan Gibson, a resident for the last fifteen years, said. “It’s a safe space for our children to hang out after school and it’s free. I would have to spend thousands of dollars every month to find someone to watch my kids after school and on the weekends.”

>But many business leaders think replacing the community centers with new shops could help improve the struggling economy. “Having more space for commercial businesses opens our city up to people from surrounding cities looking to relocate. We might see new and innovative businesses from Milwaukee, Minneapolis, and even Chicago,” Melissa Nguyen, owner of several boutiques, suggested.

>In a formal statement, Jayda Wallace, city council member, stated, “We’re looking into how we can balance the needs of the community while also stimulating the economy by opening us up to new businesses. We think there’s a way that both parties can get what they want.”


**What right did the people featured in the article use?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to assemble peacefully
right to freedom of movement
right to access education
right to refuse medical care

assessment_items.question_content:
> **Philadelphia, PA** — The race for mayor of Philadelphia is officially on! On Wednesday, former high school teacher and city council member, George Feeny announced that he would be running for the open mayoral seat. 

>In a statement to the press, Feeny said, “I’m running for mayor because I’m passionate about serving our community. As mayor, I hope to improve our city’s infrastructure and update our schools. Our school system is failing our children and needs to be changed to meet the challenges of the future.”

>We asked several constituents what they thought about Mr. Feeny’s chances in the mayoral race. One community member, Eric Matthews, said, “Feeny? Feeny! I think he’s going to do a great job! He has a great vision for our schools and I think under his leadership, we could see Philadelphia change for the better.”

**What right did Mr. Feeny use?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to run for public office
right to join a political party
right to privacy
right to freedom of religion

assessment_items.question_content:
>**Charlotte, NC** — 2020 is looking to be a year of transformation for Charlotte. This year alone, we’ve seen 145 businesses register in the city of Charlotte.

>We’ve seen applications for new food trucks, boutiques, tech startups, and more. If you’ve thought of it, there’s probably someone out there building a business plan on it.

>Why do these businesses come to Charlotte? As one business owner said, “The low cost of living and the friendly environment makes Charlotte an ideal place to start a new business.”

**What right did the people featured in the article use?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: right to establish and operate a business
right to change employment
right to organize a labor union
right to dispose of property

assessment_items.question_content:
Noor is getting treatment for her chronic back pain. Her doctor recommends that Noor get an experimental surgery to help relieve some of her pain. However, since it’s an expensive surgery and it might not work, Noor decides that she does not want to have the surgery. Her doctor insists that this is the best course of action for her pain and asks her to reconsider.

**If Noor didn’t want the surgery, what is the best action she could take?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: invoke her right to refuse medical care and ask for a different treatment plan
sue her doctor for trying to put her through an expensive surgery
apply for social security to see if it would cover the costs for her surgery
press charges against her doctor and get the police to arrest her

assessment_items.question_content:
Iris Allen-West, a reporter for the *Central City Picture,* has found some evidence that the Central City Mayor has been behind some serious crimes. As she’s getting ready to publish the story, the police commissioner tells her if she publishes the story, he’ll arrest her for harassment. She publishes it anyway.

**What right protects Iris’s ability to publish the story?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: freedom of the press
freedom of speech
right to a speedy trial
right to run for federal office

assessment_items.question_content:
Muhammad is an artist and has created an illustrative map for a fantasy book he is writing. He wants to post the map to a social media website for feedback, but he also doesn’t want anyone to steal or repurpose his work.

**If Muhammad wanted to protect his work and be able to take legal action if necessary, what action might he take?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: register his work with the government for copyright protection
sign his name in the map's corner so that no one can take it off
make a blank version of the map to make it less appealing to steal
tell the police about his plans so they can arrest anyone he tries to copy his work
